data.title: BTC price might hit $75,000-80,000 by May 2026 with current price at $68,963, neutral RSI at 50.69, and bearish MACD. Technical analysis signals mixed.
data.description: The current price of Bitcoin stands at $68,963, accompanied by a neutral Relative Strength Index (RSI) of 50.69 and a bearish Moving Average Convergence Divergence (MACD) momentum. According to technical analysis, if a crucial resistance level is breached, Bitcoin's price is anticipated to reach between $75,000 and $80,000 by May 2026.

data.title: GC Biopharma Enhances IVIG Safety Profile with Advanced Prothrombotic Impurity Detection Technology
data.description: A new proprietary analytical technique for accurate measurement of Factor XI (FXI) has been released in the Journal of Microbiology and Biotechnology. This method aims to provide precise results regarding FXI levels. Additionally, a novel IgG blocker has been introduced to eliminate interference from heterophilic antibodies. This innovation guarantees clinical-grade purity, reducing the chances of thromboembolic complications. The advancements were made in YONGIN, South Korea, in April. These developments are expected to significantly enhance the quality of medical analysis and mitigate risks associated with thromboembolism.

data.title: ETH price aims for $2,300 by May 2026. Currently at $2,122 with RSI at 52.86. Analysis sees upward move, but bearish MACD may lead to consolidation.
data.description: The cryptocurrency Ethereum is currently trading at $2,122 with a neutral Relative Strength Index (RSI) standing at 52.86. Analysts conducting technical analysis predict a possible surge in value towards the $2,300 resistance level. However, the bearish Moving Average Convergence Divergence (MACD) indicator signals a potential period of consolidation in the near future.

data.title: US Stocks Mixed Following Trump's Iran War Address: Investor Fear Eases, But Greed Index Remains In 'Extreme Fear' Zone
data.description: The CNN Money Fear and Greed index showed a slight decrease in overall fear levels, remaining in the "Extreme Fear" zone on Thursday. U.S. stocks had a mixed performance, with the Dow Jones index dropping approximately 0.1% following comments from President Donald Trump. Iran is said to be working on a monitoring protocol with Oman for the Strait of Hormuz. Tesla Inc. (NASDAQ: TSLA) saw a significant decline of over 5% to a seven-month low due to weak first-quarter delivery figures. Acuity Inc. (NYSE: AYI) experienced an 8% decrease in its stock price after reporting mixed fiscal second-quarter 2026 results. For more information, the full story is available on Benzinga.com.
Iranian reports say buildings at Sharif University of Technology in Tehran were hit during U.S.-Israeli strikes, and authorities are monitoring the aftermath.
Tel Aviv, Ramat Gan, and Bnei Brak report the fall of fragments from an Iranian rocket.

data.title: Apple Pulls Down Jack Dorsey's Bitchat Messaging Service From China's App Store For Containing 'Illegal' Content
data.description: Apple, under the direction of Chinese authorities, has removed the messaging service Bitchat from the App Store. The CEO of Block Inc. (NYSE: XYZ), Jack Dorsey, disclosed this development on Sunday. The Cyberspace Administration of China cited the presence of "illegal" content as the reason for the delisting. Apple issued a notification stating that apps must comply with all legal requirements in each location they are offered. The company emphasized the importance of adhering to local laws and ensuring app compliance. Although the app is no longer available in China, it remains accessible in other regions. Apple recently reduced App Store fees for developers in China amidst rising regulatory challenges.

data.title: TP Receives Frost & Sullivan's 2026 India Company of the Year Recognition for Excellence in Customer Experience Management Services
data.description: TP, previously known as Teleperformance, has been acknowledged for its prominence in digital transformation, responsible AI integration, and outcome-focused outsourced services. The announcement was made on April 6, 2026, in SAN ANTONIO by Frost & Sullivan. This recognition underscores TP's position as a global leader in digital services.
